matching_rule: One base case matches when a promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample. Three additional case families match when the whitelist mean beta, the gene-name-related mean beta, or the all-numeric mean beta resolves to low, medium, or high methylation. Variant prediction rules receive the decoded sample GT, zygosity, ALT dosage, and site REF -> ALT definition; curated allele rules take precedence only when the sample genotype carries the relevant allele dosage.
disclaimer: Predictive theses in this database are literature-guided research summaries derived from the bundled gene interpretation bundle. They are designed for exploratory synthesis in the UI and should not be treated as diagnostic or therapeutic claims.
concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a GSDMB/17q12-q21 thesis: the individual may carry a research-grade marker relevant to gasdermin B, epithelial immune biology, asthma, or linked ORMDL3/GSDMB regulatory haplotypes. Separate direct GSDMB protein hypotheses from linked-locus expression effects.

variant_prediction_rules.prediction: GT-confirmed rs11078927 dosage suggests this sample carries the GSDMB prediction context described by the gene-level thesis. The variant observed in this sample suggests a GSDMB/17q12-q21 thesis: the individual may carry a research-grade marker relevant to gasdermin B, epithelial immune biology, asthma, or linked ORMDL3/GSDMB regulatory haplotypes. Separate direct GSDMB protein hypotheses from linked-locus expression effects. This specific marker is curated as GSDMB splice/coding asthma-locus marker. rs11078927 is bundled as a GSDMB research marker for allergic disease, asthma, eczema, airway inflammation, or immune-regulatory cohort literature. Treat it as low-penetrance, cohort-aware association context rather than as a diagnostic or treatment-selection allele.
variant_prediction_rules.sample_change_template: This sample matched rs11078927 at site definition {change} with GT {gt_raw} decoded as {genotype} ({zygosity}). Anchor the GSDMB prediction to genotype dosage {allele_dosage} rather than REF -> ALT presence alone. Dosage-specific conclusions should be softened when GT, depth, balance, or genotype quality are uncertain.
variant_prediction_rules.basis: May tag regulatory, expression, coding, haplotype, or linked-locus variation around GSDMB.

methylation_sources.description: Uses the curated whitelist probes stored in the interpretation database for the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: Gene-name-related mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses rows whose gene annotation explicitly names the current gene.

methylation_sources.description: Uses every numeric beta value that survived preprocessing for the current sample.

cases.label: Gene variant found
cases.prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a GSDMB/17q12-q21 thesis: the individual may carry a research-grade marker relevant to gasdermin B, epithelial immune biology, asthma, or linked ORMDL3/GSDMB regulatory haplotypes. Separate direct GSDMB protein hypotheses from linked-locus expression effects. An observed GSDMB variant places this sample in the curated GSDMB research context. The bundled GSDMB knowledge base is focused on asthma, allergy, atopic dermatitis, airway inflammation, epithelial-barrier biology, and immune-regulatory research. Seeded markers and locus models are intended as research context and should not be promoted to diagnostic, prognostic, or treatment-selection claims without external clinical curation. GSDMB variants can tag coding, splicing, and expression effects within the ORMDL3/GSDMB asthma haplotype block.
cases.rationale: This is the base thesis that activates as soon as any promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample.

cases.label: Gene variant found + high whitelist mean beta
cases.prediction: When a GSDMB variant is paired with high methylation in the whitelist mean beta, the sample best fits a combined regulatory-context thesis. This pattern leans toward stronger regulatory restraint or reduced local accessibility around the locus. The variant side of the thesis is: The variant observed in this sample suggests a GSDMB/17q12-q21 thesis: the individual may carry a research-grade marker relevant to gasdermin B, epithelial immune biology, asthma, or linked ORMDL3/GSDMB regulatory haplotypes. Separate direct GSDMB protein hypotheses from linked-locus expression effects. GSDMB methylation should be read as local regulatory context around an allergic-inflammation gene. Promoter-proximal or gene-body beta values may help frame accessibility or expression hypotheses, but they are not validated stand-alone biomarkers for asthma, eczema, allergy, immune activation, or treatment response. The bundled GSDMB knowledge base is focused on asthma, allergy, atopic dermatitis, airway inflammation, epithelial-barrier biology, and immune-regulatory research. Seeded markers and locus models are intended as research context and should not be promoted to diagnostic, prognostic, or treatment-selection claims without external clinical curation. GSDMB variants can tag coding, splicing, and expression effects within the ORMDL3/GSDMB asthma haplotype block.
